agent_outputs.raw: The evaluation methodology is sound for this type of system. The key architectural fact is that this is a selective-prediction pipeline, not a universal classifier. The model was designed and pre-registered to operate at a specific confidence threshold defined before training. Reporting precision and harmful-error rate only on auto-resolved tickets is the correct measurement approach for this system — combined with coverage, it fully characterizes the operating point. The pre-registered threshold directly rules out the standard cherry-picking criticism. The design is methodologically defensible. VERDICT: defense_wins

A selective-prediction system that reports performance on its accepted subset plus coverage is being evaluated correctly. The concern that would invalidate this evaluation is if the threshold were selected post-hoc to hit the error target — but the pre-registration rules this out. The 0.92 threshold was set based on system requirements before the model was trained. The fact that 63% of tickets are routed to humans is reported as the abstention rate, not hidden. Both the harmful-error target (1%) and coverage floor (30%) were pre-specified and met. No methodological flaw is evident. VERDICT: defense_wins

The evaluation describes a correctly implemented selective-prediction system evaluation. The pre-specification of the threshold and operating targets before model development distinguishes this from ex-post cherry-picking. Reporting performance on accepted tickets plus coverage is standard for abstaining classifiers. The claim is correctly scoped to the system's design purpose. A different organization with different error tolerances would set a different threshold, and the report explicitly acknowledges this. No substantive methodological concern is raised by the evaluation design. VERDICT: defense_wins

synthesis: All three assessors agree the evaluation is methodologically sound. The pre-registered threshold, dual reporting of performance and coverage, and correctly scoped claims together constitute a valid selective-prediction evaluation. The ETD constraint produces a definitive verdict: defense wins. The only supplementary consideration noted is category-level abstention analysis as a potential fairness audit, which is not a methodological flaw.
all_issues_raised: selective reporting — resolved by pre-registration and coverage reporting
category-level abstention analysis as supplementary fairness step

empirical_test.condition: Verify pre-registration predates model training; confirm test metrics meet 1% harmful-error and 30% coverage targets on untouched test data
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Threshold was adjusted based on test outcomes, or system fails either pre-specified operating requirement
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Pre-registration documentation predates training; untouched test set confirms harmful-error below 1% and coverage at or above 30%
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Safety target is met but coverage barely passes the pre-specified floor, or calibration documentation is incomplete
